title: festuca arenaria subsp. oraria
intro: Festuca arenaria subsp. oraria, also known as the sea fescue or coastal fescue, is a subspecies of grass that is native to the coastal regions of Europe and North Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The plant is rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. Festuca arenaria subsp. oraria can be prepared into various herbal remedies, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and other respiratory issues.
